questions: how is BERT different from GPT?
BERT NLP te kivabe use hoy?
answers: BERT stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. It reads text in both directions simultaneously, giving it a deeper understanding of context than left-to-right models.
BERT holo Google er ekta language model ja bidirectional context bujhte pare. Eta masked language modeling use kore train hoy — kisu word hide kore baki theke predict kore.

BERT is a pre-trained transformer model excellent for tasks like question answering, text classification, and named entity recognition. Unlike GPT which generates text, BERT is designed for understanding text.

BERT revolutionized NLP when Google released it in 2018. It's an encoder-only transformer that learns contextual word representations, making it powerful for downstream classification and extraction tasks.
